tldr: NATS (open-source pub/sub) and Plivo (hosted SMS/voice/WhatsApp API) are different categories, so this only makes sense as a refactor of any NATS-driven flows that were really trying to reach end users via SMS or voice.
why_switch: Plivo provides managed SMS, voice, and WhatsApp APIs out of the box
Public per-message pricing and a freemium starting tier
No broker to operate for the messaging-to-users path
Global carrier connectivity NATS cannot provide on its own
what_to_export: NATS subjects/consumers tied to user-facing SMS or voice notifications
Phone number lists and any consent records
Routing logic by country or carrier
Monitoring, retries, and dead-letter handling logic
Internal flows that must remain on NATS
export_method: NATS has no vendor export — drain or replay JetStream streams via the NATS CLI or client libraries if relevant, and capture subject/consumer topology from your own configuration and IaC. Consult NATS's official docs for current tooling.
import_method: On Plivo, create an account, buy or port the numbers you need, generate API credentials, and call Plivo's SMS or Voice API from the services that previously published to NATS for end-user messaging. Configure webhooks for delivery receipts and inbound messages. Consult Plivo's official docs for current setup steps.
gotchas: Different categories — internal pub/sub should stay on NATS
Telecom compliance (opt-in, opt-out, sender ID rules) varies by country
Per-message cost can dominate at scale
No broker-style replay; design retries and idempotency yourself
Number provisioning and porting can take time
Latency and ordering guarantees differ from a message bus

email.subject: Moving user-facing SMS/voice from NATS-driven flows to Plivo
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposal: where NATS is currently used to drive user-facing SMS or voice notifications, switch those flows to Plivo. Internal pub/sub stays on NATS.

Scope: audit the relevant subjects, set up a Plivo account and numbers, wire SMS/voice API calls and delivery webhooks, and add retries/idempotency on our side. Estimated 1-3 days.

Will coordinate compliance review (opt-in/opt-out) before cutover.

Thanks
